title: Visual Reply Controls
description: A walkthrough of visual-reply settings, the Visual Reply dock, and the master automatic image-generation policy.

steps.title: Open Host -> Visuals
steps.body: This page controls image generation backends, size, model, and dock behavior. It is the normal home for Visual Reply configuration.

steps.title: Mode
steps.body: Visual Reply Mode controls whether NC can generate images at all. If Mode is Off, NC will not ask the model for [visualize: ...] tags or generate images automatically.

steps.title: Provider and Model
steps.body: These controls choose the image backend and model. Use them when you want to route visual replies through OpenAI or xAI / Grok and adjust the exact image model in use.

steps.title: Auto-Show Dock
steps.body: This controls whether the Visual Reply dock automatically comes forward when image generation starts or finishes.

steps.title: Open Host -> Vision
steps.body: The final piece of the puzzle lives in Host -> Vision, because hidden sensory behavior can also lead to image generation requests.

steps.title: Master Auto-Visual Switch
steps.body: This toggle is the master policy for spontaneous image generation. When it is off, NC should ignore both hidden PONG image requests and ordinary assistant [visualize: ...] tags.

steps.title: Show Visual Reply Dock
steps.body: This button opens the Visual Reply dock manually. It is useful when you want to inspect the last image, change captions, or confirm what NC generated.

steps.title: What This Means in Practice
steps.body: Think of the policy as two layers: Visuals chooses whether image generation exists at all, and the master auto-visual toggle chooses whether NC may do it spontaneously without you manually requesting an image.
